Tamheed v2.3 migration data repair, 2026-07-22/23. Recovered the discarded roadmap as DOC-053, corrected 8 deferred-work statuses to match the v1 register, rewrote the three handoff prompts to the v2 protocol, restored 155 WBS rows with hyphens and full titles, backfilled 125 dated progress entries with three-valued date provenance, and created PH-4 under DEC-029 and SC-001. Repair driven from the DEF- ledger; extraction tooling and the pre/post null-rate reconciliation are committed under tools/tamheed-repair/.

Migration data repair complete for this pass. Fixed and verified: DEF-004 typed-column starvation, DEF-005 identifier renumbering (crosswalk in SEC-920; ids are FK-referenced primary keys so the drift is mitigated, not undone), DEF-006 degenerate epic titles, DEF-007 the discarded roadmap (DOC-053), DEF-009 the stale and broken handoff prompts, DEF-010 status defaulting, DEF-011 risk state in the wrong column. DEF-013 closed as will-not-fix: acceptance criteria were set Approved before slices existed, so the immutability trigger blocks slice_id permanently and v_phase_exit can never return non-zero.

Remaining after this pass, deliberately left open rather than claimed. DEF-001, DEF-002 and DEF-003 are only partly closed: the 155 WBS rows were fully re-sourced from the frozen archive, but 78 requirement statements are still truncated at 120 characters with hyphens stripped, because they came through the same lossy title-fallback path and their recovery needs a separate pass over the functional-requirements and user-story files. DEF-008 (the 82622-character progress-log Preamble that buries 23 dated entries) and DEF-012 (the derived backlog, triaged on 23 rows of evidence with 132 left untouched rather than assumed) also remain open. Per-slice work_bind was skipped: stamping last_referenced on slices that already carry Implemented status, objectives and progress entries adds no information and would inject today-dated rows into a carefully dated history.

Process finding for the package itself, recorded in DOC-054 section SEC-921. The v2.3 migration reported seven of seven gates passing over every defect listed in the DEF- family, because all seven gates are row-level and the damage was column-level: a row that exists, with a well-formed identifier and no placeholder text, passes every one regardless of whether a typed column was silently left empty or a title was cut at a fixed cap. The two checks that would have caught eight of the thirteen classes are per-column null-rate profiling and a field-length histogram flagging mass at exactly the cap; both are implemented in tools/tamheed-repair/profile_nulls.py with the pre-repair baseline committed beside the post-repair run.
